log.c(3) Library Functions Manual log.c(3)

NAME

log.c - Logging utility stubs for WASM.

SYNOPSIS

Typedefs


typedef struct session_log_buffer session_log_buffer_t
Opaque handle to log buffer instance.

Functions


asciichat_error_t log_mmap_init_simple (const char *log_path, size_t max_size)
Initialize mmap logging with simple parameters. bool log_mmap_is_active (void)
Check if mmap logging is active. void log_mmap_write (int level, const char *file, int line, const char *func, const char *fmt,...)
Write a log entry directly to the mmap'd file (lock-free). bool log_mmap_get_usage (size_t *used, size_t *capacity)
Get current mmap log usage. void log_mmap_rotate (void)
Rotate the mmap log (tail-keeping rotation). void server_status_log_append (const char *message)
void log_json_write (int fd, int level, uint64_t time_ns, const char *file, int line, const char *func, const char *json_msg)
int terminal_choose_log_fd (log_level_t level)
Choose output file descriptor for logging based on level and interactivity. session_log_buffer_t * session_log_buffer_create (void)
Create a new session log buffer instance. void session_log_buffer_destroy (session_log_buffer_t *buf)
Cleanup session log buffer instance. void session_log_buffer_clear (session_log_buffer_t *buf)
Clear all log entries from buffer. void session_log_buffer_append (session_log_buffer_t *buf, const char *message)
Append a log message to the buffer. size_t session_log_buffer_get_recent (session_log_buffer_t *buf, session_log_entry_t *out_entries, size_t max_count)
Get recent log entries from buffer. void log_mmap_destroy (void)
Shutdown mmap logging.

Detailed Description

Logging utility stubs for WASM.

Definition in file platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.

Function Documentation

void log_json_write (int fd, int level, uint64_t time_ns, const char * file, int line, const char * func, const char * json_msg)

Definition at line 51 of file platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.

52                                           {
53   (void)fd;
54   (void)level;
55   (void)time_ns;
56   (void)file;
57   (void)line;
58   (void)func;
59   (void)json_msg;
60   // No-op - JSON logging in WASM uses console output instead
61 }








void
  log_mmap_destroy (void )


Shutdown mmap logging. Syncs and unmaps the log file.


Definition at line 103 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


103                             {
104   // No-op - mmap logging not used in WASM
105 }










bool log_mmap_get_usage (size_t * used, size_t *
  capacity)


Get current mmap log usage.


Parameters


used Current bytes used in the log


capacity Total capacity of the log


Returns


true if mmap is active and values are valid


Definition at line 32 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


32                                                         {
33   if (used)
34     *used = 0;
35   if (capacity)
36     *capacity = 0;
37   return false;
38 }










asciichat_error_t log_mmap_init_simple (const char *
  log_path, size_t max_size)


Initialize mmap logging with simple parameters. Convenience
    wrapper around log_mmap_init().




Parameters


log_path Path to log file


max_size Maximum file size (0 = default 4MB)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 13 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


13                                                                               {
14   (void)log_path;
15   (void)max_size;
16   return ASCIICHAT_OK; // No-op - mmap logging not supported in WASM
17 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK.






bool log_mmap_is_active (void )


Check if mmap logging is active.


Returns


true if mmap logging is initialized and active


Definition at line 19 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


19                               {
20   return false; // mmap logging not active in WASM
21 }










void
  log_mmap_rotate (void )


Rotate the mmap log (tail-keeping rotation). Keeps the most recent
    log entries (tail) and discards old ones. This is the mmap equivalent of
    file-based log rotation.




Note


Caller must hold the rotation mutex from logging.c. This
  is called by maybe_rotate_log() which handles locking.


Definition at line 40 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


40                            {
41   // No-op
42 }










void
  log_mmap_write (int level, const char * file, int line, const char * func,
  const char * fmt, ...)


Write a log entry directly to the mmap'd file (lock-free). Formats
    the log message and writes it directly as human-readable text. Uses atomic
    operations only, no mutex.




Parameters


level Log level


file Source file (can be NULL)


line Source line


func Function name (can be NULL)


fmt Format string


... Format arguments


Definition at line 23 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


23                                                                                                    {
24   (void)level;
25   (void)file;
26   (void)line;
27   (void)func;
28   (void)fmt;
29   // No-op - mmap logging not supported in WASM
30 }










void
  server_status_log_append (const char * message)


Definition at line 45 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


45                                                    {
46   (void)message;
47   // No-op - server status screen not used in WASM mirror mode
48 }










void
  session_log_buffer_append (session_log_buffer_t * buf, const char *
  message)


Append a log message to the buffer. Thread-safe. Called from
    logging system to capture messages. Message is copied into circular buffer
    (oldest entry overwritten if full).




Parameters


buf Buffer instance (must not be NULL)


message Log message text (already formatted with colors)


Definition at line 88 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


88                                                                                {
89   (void)buf;
90   (void)message;
91   // No-op - session log buffer not used in WASM
92 }










void
  session_log_buffer_clear (session_log_buffer_t * buf)


Clear all log entries from buffer. Thread-safe. Resets buffer to
    empty state, discarding all captured logs. Useful for clearing
    initialization logs before screen starts rendering.




Parameters


buf Buffer instance (must not be NULL)


Definition at line 83 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


83                                                          {
84   (void)buf;
85   // No-op - session log buffer not used in WASM
86 }






Referenced by terminal_screen_log_clear().






session_log_buffer_t * session_log_buffer_create (void
  )


Create a new session log buffer instance. Allocates and
    initializes a circular buffer for log capture. Caller is responsible for
    calling session_log_buffer_destroy() when done.




Returns


Pointer to new buffer instance, or NULL on allocation
  failure


Definition at line 74 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


74                                                       {
75   return NULL;  // No-op - session log buffer not used in WASM
76 }






Referenced by terminal_screen_log_init().






void
  session_log_buffer_destroy (session_log_buffer_t * buf)


Cleanup session log buffer instance. Frees the buffer and all
    internal resources. Safe to call with NULL.




Parameters


buf Buffer instance to destroy (can be NULL)


Definition at line 78 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


78                                                            {
79   (void)buf;
80   // No-op - session log buffer not used in WASM
81 }






Referenced by terminal_screen_log_destroy().






size_t
  session_log_buffer_get_recent (session_log_buffer_t * buf,
  session_log_entry_t * out_entries, size_t max_count)


Get recent log entries from buffer. Thread-safe. Copies up to
    max_count recent entries into out_entries. Entries are returned in
    chronological order (oldest first).




Parameters


buf Buffer instance (must not be NULL)


out_entries Output array to fill with log entries


max_count Maximum number of entries to return


Returns


Number of entries actually copied (may be less than
  max_count)


Definition at line 94 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


96                                                         {
97   (void)buf;
98   (void)out_entries;
99   (void)max_count;
100   return 0; // No entries in WASM
101 }






Referenced by log_search_gather_and_filter_logs(), and
    terminal_screen_render().






int
  terminal_choose_log_fd (log_level_t level)


Choose output file descriptor for logging based on level and
    interactivity.


Parameters


level Log level (determines default routing: WARN+
  to stderr, others to stdout)


Returns


STDERR_FILENO or STDOUT_FILENO based on log level and
  terminal state


Routes logs appropriately:


    
  
  • When terminal is NOT interactive (piped): ALL logs to stderr
    • 
  
  • When force_stderr enabled: ALL logs to stderr
    • 
  
  • Otherwise: WARN/ERROR/FATAL to stderr, others to stdout
    • 



This consolidates log routing logic used throughout the
  codebase.




Note


Use: int fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(LOG_INFO);



Common usage: platform_write_all(terminal_choose_log_fd(level),
    data, len);




Definition at line 64 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


64                                               {
65   (void)level;
66   return 2; // Stub: return stderr-like fd
67 }
